content: Just as linguistic reflection (the common English lexical meaning of reflexion) describes how a surface mirrors an external object and how humans reflect on their own thinking to adjust beliefs, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the layered structure of human linguistic reflexion: a first pass of reflecting on the external task output, followed by a second pass of reflecting on the LLM’s own internal reasoning process itself. This dual-layer reflexion framework can reduce inconsistent reasoning that plagues current single-layer LLM self-reflection approaches.
